task_completion.visual_evidence: The AttendHRM application window is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the manual attendance entry or timesheet section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The AttendHRM interface shows the 'Attendance', 'Manual Entry', 'Timesheet', or equivalent screen for recording employee hours.

task_completion.description: Select or search for the employee 'Robert Johnson' (EMP003)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'Robert Johnson' or ID 'EMP003' is typed into an employee search bar, selection dropdown, or employee field within the attendance entry screen.

task_completion.description: Set the attendance date to January 15, 2025
task_completion.visual_evidence: The date field for the attendance record is explicitly set to '15/01/2025', 'Jan 15, 2025', or an equivalent format representing January 15, 2025.

task_completion.description: Enter the work hours (09:00 to 18:00) and save the record
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'In' time is set to 09:00 and the 'Out' time is set to 18:00. There should be evidence of the agent clicking 'Save', 'Apply', or the record appearing in a list of saved attendance entries.

integrity.description: The agent used the AttendHRM graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the AttendHRM application windows and menus, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly modifying the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No database management tools (like FlameRobin or DBeaver) or SQL scripts are visible being used to insert the attendance record directly into the Firebird database.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual AttendHRM software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application being used has the Lenvica AttendHRM branding and standard interface elements, not a generic text editor or spreadsheet mimicking the task.

privileged_info_for_vlm: AttendHRM is an HRMS product by Lenvica. The default demo database typically uses numeric or hyphenated employee IDs (e.g., 108, EMP-1024). The requested employee 'Robert Johnson' (EMP003) may not exist in the default database. If the agent encounters a 'not found' error when searching for the employee but has correctly navigated the UI and inputted the target data (name/ID, date, and times) as far as the application allows, this should still be considered positive progress for the respective steps.
